claim_results.claim: Every act of divine catastrophe in the Hebrew scriptures — the Flood, Sodom, the Canaanite wars, the Exile — has been called evidence that God is violent, capricious, or tribal.
The church declared him a heretic but never fully answered the structural question underneath his claim.
The Missing Protection Layer In the Master Equation, every human being has a coupling constant to the coherence field.
Conscious observers participate in the equation through their own $C$-state, not as a separate consciousness term. $C$ isn't binary; it has a magnitude.
He gets to you through the structures you depend on — because that's the only route still available.
The armor exists because the attack mode changed.
God doing triage — external catastrophic intervention — because the internal repair mechanism doesn't exist yet.
Because the operating conditions require it.

The Holy Spirit couldn't be distributed before Pentecost not because of divine preference but because of structure : the accumulated entropy of human sin — the S term in the equation — blocked permanent Spirit-to-human bonding.
Because what the operating conditions permitted changed.
The Holy Spirit installed as the protection layer changes what God must do, because it changes what the threat can do.
Claim 1 · Post-Pentecost If this framework is correct, post-Pentecost adversarial strategy should be systematically institutional — working through structures, not direct personal encounter.
Claim 2 · Pre-Pentecost Pre-Pentecost adversarial accounts should be systematically personal — direct entity-to-human contact, no institutional intermediary required.
Any claim that turns a structural resemblance into a proven mechanism belongs here until the math, experiment, or textual chain can carry the weight.
Possible overstatement: language that sounds final should be read as a working model unless the article explicitly proves it.
The coherence equation reads it differently: when an entire population's coherence drops below the recovery threshold, the system cannot self-correct.
The equation predicts a critical threshold below which no amount of internal effort can reverse decay.
The structural claim: The Flood was not arbitrary violence.
The equation predicts when that threshold is crossed.
Rigor & Kill Conditions Every claim is held to explicit falsification standards.
This article is mostly structural rather than analytical — the formal coherence equation appears in Articles 04, 05, and 09.
Direct entity-to-observer contact, possible because the protection layer is absent and the human's coherence channel is exposed.

Passive $$\text{Election: G acts} \Rightarrow \text{active} \quad|\quad \text{Catastrophe: } \frac{dC}{dt} = -S \cdot C \;\text{within network} \Rightarrow \text{passive end-state}$$ The article notes the same asymmetry developed in Article 05: salvation requires external input; catastrophe occurs when input is absent and decay is allowed to run to its terminus.
In the Master Equation, a per-node parameter governing how strongly each human is coupled to the coherence field.
The framework's foundational substrate — the χ field of the Master Equation that human observers couple to via their coherence state (the C variable).
The Master-Equation field whose value is total system coherence.
Framework note: Entropy (physical frame) and Sin (theological frame) name the same Master-Equation variable; the notation is $S$ either way.
Read structurally: armor exists because the attack mode changed.
The article's central image: the OT surgeon amputates because penicillin hasn't been invented yet.

The article's empirical handle: pre-Pentecost adversarial accounts should be systematically personal; post-Pentecost should be systematically institutional. “Read the text and count.” The distribution is offered as testable.
The article's central claim: the OT and NT are the same God acting under structurally different operating conditions.
Adam in Eden, Noah at the Flood, Christ at the Cross. (Defined in detail in Article 01.) Coherence (C) Alignment with the Logos source — the C variable in the Master Equation.
